title: viguiera anchusifolia var. immarginata
intro: Viguiera anchusifolia var. immarginata, also known as the Immarginate Viguiera, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a shrubby perennial with medicinal properties due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, thanks to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Viguiera anchusifolia var. immarginata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for internal use, and its leaves can be applied topically to affected areas for direct application.
